bridge_concepts: FDX1 ferredoxin as Cu2+ reductase (E0' -274 mV) vs Cu2+/Cu+ (E0 +159 mV)
DLAT lipoyl moiety Cu binding (Kd ~10^-17) vs CuS covellite (Ksp ~10^-36)
Cu+/Cu2+ Pourbaix speciation at mitochondrial conditions
Chalcopyrite CuFeS2 as structural analog for Cu-Fe-S interactions
Copper chaperones (CCS, ATOX1, COX17) pathway completeness

checks.name: thermodynamic_feasibility
checks.detail: At mito Eh (-300 mV), Cu+ favored 2.88e7:1. FDX1 delta_G = -41.8 kJ/mol but thermodynamically redundant. FDX1 is kinetically essential, not thermodynamically driving.
checks.warning: Do NOT claim FDX1 drives Cu2+ reduction thermodynamically. Frame as kinetic facilitator.

checks.name: pourbaix_diagram_validity
checks.detail: Cu+ stability field in both mito (10^7-10^8) and vents (10^9-10^12). Speciation identical. CuS/Cu2S stable when sulfide present. Overlap zone at Eh ~-300 mV, pH 8-9.
checks.warning: Ligand-extended Pourbaix diagrams are hypothetical. Standard Cu-H2O Pourbaix is established.

checks.name: concentration_compatibility
checks.detail: Cuproptosis threshold (~10 uM) matches vent Cu (1-100 uM). Both organic-ligand buffered. Strongest quantitative bridge.

checks.name: fe_s_cluster_displacement
checks.detail: K(displacement) = 10^7.5, delta_G = -44.5 kJ/mol. Cu+ displaces Fe2+ at picomolar concentrations. Macomber & Imlay 2009 (PMID 19416816) provides direct experimental confirmation. Chalcopyrite analogy valid at molecular level.
checks.warning: Chalcopyrite analogy valid for bonding geometry only, not crystallinity or temperature.

checks.name: pubmed_cooccurrence
checks.detail: cuproptosis AND Pourbaix: 0. FDX1 AND geochemistry: 0. cuproptosis AND hydrothermal: 3 (irrelevant nanoparticle papers). Zero papers apply Eh-pH speciation to cuproptosis.

checks.name: back_of_envelope
checks.detail: ES-Cu Ka (10^17.1) ~ lipoyl-Cu Ka (10^17) - comparable binding explains delivery. CuS Ksp (10^-36) is massive thermodynamic sink. Cu2+ hydrolysis at pH 8: 76% as CuOH+.
checks.warning: CuS formation requires free sulfide (pathological, not basal). Testable by TEM/EDX.

notes.cuproptosis_hydrothermal_3: Nanoparticle synthesis methods, not vent geochemistry
notes.key_paper: Macomber & Imlay 2009 (PMID 19416816) - Cu+ destroys Fe-S clusters in dehydratases

overall_rationale: All 6 checks PLAUSIBLE. Cu speciation identical in both environments. Concentration regimes match. Fe-S displacement thermodynamically validated with literature support. Fields confirmed DISJOINT. Main risk: overstatement of thermodynamic framing (FDX1 paradox).
warnings_for_generator: FDX1 is kinetically necessary, NOT thermodynamically driving Cu2+ reduction - do not overstate
Ligand-extended Pourbaix diagrams are hypothetical (session evaluation condition)
Chalcopyrite analogy valid at molecular bonding level only - not crystallographic
CuS formation in mitochondria requires free sulfide release (pathological, not basal)
Concentration match (10 uM) is the strongest quantitative bridge - emphasize
Cite Macomber & Imlay 2009 (PMID 19416816) for Fe-S displacement - strongest published support
